g.text: Noisy Neighbor — pool mode's defining failure
One tenant's runaway workload consumes most of the shared resource. Mitigations layer; they bound the worst case but never eliminate it.
Pool mode — one shared resource, 200 tenants. Tenant T-42 runs an unbounded report and saturates the resource.
Result: 199 tenants see 5-second query times for 10 minutes.
Anchor: 80% of CPU stolen by 1 of 200 tenants. In pool mode this is a structural failure, not a bug.
Cap requests/sec at the API edge per tenant.
Bounds the WORST CASE at the request layer.
Does NOT protect against single expensive queries.
Bound DB connections, CPU, IOPS per tenant.
Bounds the WORST CASE at the resource layer.
Requires DB or platform that supports per-tenant accounting.
Partition shared infra; T-42 only touches its shard.
T-42's noise only affects its shard's tenants.
Strongest mitigation. Turns pure pool into
something closer to bridge for resource scheduling.
Reality: the problem can be BOUNDED, not eliminated. Customers that cannot tolerate any noise must be moved to bridge or silo.
